channel.title: regex on Geoff Dixon > Write-Verbose
channel.description: Recent content in regex on Geoff Dixon > Write-Verbose

item.title: PowerShell Toolmaking via RegEx
item.description: This post shows how to create a PowerShell Get-WLAN function by parsing the output of the built-in Windows NETSH.EXE PowerShell Tools are re-usable functions that can be used stand-alone or in a pipeline Ideally, the function is written in pure PowerShell, but sometimes its more convenient to create a wrapper script using the output of a command line tool. The example below creates a PowerShell tool to get information about WI-Fi connections on the local computer.
